title: Jobinia neei
taxonomy: Jobinia neei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Jobinia and is a part of the Apocynaceae family. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, often referred to as dogbanes or oleanders. The plant is native to the Americas, specifically in the tropical and subtropical regions. Jobinia neei is further classified within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which comprises many species of milkweeds and dogbanes.

intro: Jobinia neei, also known as Jobinia, is a flowering plant species native to South America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, providing potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its possible role in alleviating symptoms of anxiety and depression. Bioactive constituents of Jobinia neei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Jobinia neei include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments in folk medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
